Biblical scholars have recently had interesting things to say about a person named Malchiah. Jeremiah 38:6 speaks of a "dungeon of Malchiah the son of Hammelech . . . in the court of the prison." But the Hebrew name here, Malki Yahu ben-ham Melek, should be translated "Malki Yahu, son of the king," the Hebrew word melek meaning "king." (Robert F. Smith in John W. Welch, ed., Reexploring the Book…
